description: Bootstrap Next.js 15 project with TypeScript strict, SCSS, zustand, react-hook-form, zod, react-i18next
steps: Run: npx create-next-app@latest . --typescript --app --no-tailwind --no-src-dir --import-alias '@/*'
Install: npm install zustand react-hook-form zod react-i18next i18next
Install dev: npm install -D sass eslint-config-prettier stylelint stylelint-config-standard-scss
Configure tsconfig.json with strict: true
Setup .eslintrc.json with @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any: error and no-unused-vars: error
Setup .stylelintrc.json extending stylelint-config-standard-scss
Setup prettier.config.js
Verify: npm run build exits 0

description: Setup i18n with react-i18next — EN + RU locale files and provider
steps: Create locales/en/withdraw.json and locales/ru/withdraw.json with all UI string keys
Create src/shared/i18n/config.ts initializing i18next with react-i18next
Create src/shared/i18n/I18nProvider.tsx client component wrapping children with I18nextProvider
Add I18nProvider to app/layout.tsx
Export from src/shared/i18n/index.ts
Verify: no hardcoded user-visible strings in any .tsx file

description: Setup design system via ui-ux-pro-max — fintech crypto dark SCSS tokens
steps: Run: python3 .agent/skills/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py 'fintech crypto withdrawal form dark professional' --design-system --persist -p 'Withdraw' --page 'withdraw'
Create src/app/globals.scss with CSS custom properties from design system output (colors, spacing, radii, shadows)
Create src/app/typography.scss importing Google Fonts and defining font variables
Import both in app/layout.tsx
Verify: npm run build exits 0, fonts load in browser

description: Mock API route: POST /v1/withdrawals and GET /v1/withdrawals/[id]
steps: Create app/api/v1/withdrawals/route.ts handling POST with idempotency_key deduplication in-memory Map
POST: validate body {amount, destination, confirm}, return 201 with {id, amount, destination, status:'pending'}, return 409 if same idempotency_key seen within 5 min
Create app/api/v1/withdrawals/[id]/route.ts handling GET, returns the withdrawal record or 404
Add artificial 800ms delay to simulate network
Export types: WithdrawalRequest, WithdrawalResponse from src/shared/types/api.ts
Verify: curl POST returns 201, second identical POST returns 409, GET returns record

description: Withdrawal API client layer with idempotency_key generation and retry logic
steps: Create src/features/withdraw/api/withdrawalApi.ts
Implement createWithdrawal(payload, key): calls POST /v1/withdrawals, throws typed ApiError on 4xx/5xx
Implement getWithdrawal(id): calls GET /v1/withdrawals/{id}
idempotency_key = crypto.randomUUID() generated once per form session, persisted in store
On network error (fetch throws), propagate as NetworkError so UI can offer retry
On 409, propagate as ConflictError with message for user display
Export from src/features/withdraw/api/index.ts
No token in localStorage — auth is mocked (describe prod approach in README)

description: Zustand store: withdraw feature state — idle/loading/success/error + idempotency_key
steps: Create src/features/withdraw/store/useWithdrawStore.ts
State shape: { status: 'idle'|'loading'|'success'|'error', error: string|null, withdrawal: WithdrawalResponse|null, idempotencyKey: string }
Actions: submit(payload), retry(), reset()
submit: set loading, call createWithdrawal, on success set withdrawal+status='success', on ConflictError set error with 409 message, on NetworkError set error with retry flag
idempotencyKey initialized once via crypto.randomUUID(), regenerated only after success/reset
Export from src/features/withdraw/store/index.ts
Verify: store unit test — submit transitions idle→loading→success

description: Withdraw form validation schema with zod + react-hook-form
steps: Create src/features/withdraw/utils/withdrawSchema.ts
Schema: z.object({ amount: z.number().positive(), destination: z.string().min(1), confirm: z.literal(true) })
All error messages use i18n keys (no hardcoded strings)
Export WithdrawSchema type and schema from src/features/withdraw/utils/index.ts
Verify: schema rejects amount<=0, empty destination, unchecked confirm

description: Build WithdrawForm component — fields, validation, submission, double-submit guard
steps: Create src/features/withdraw/components/WithdrawForm.tsx
Use useForm<WithdrawSchema> with zodResolver
Fields: amount (number input, >0), destination (text input), confirm (checkbox)
Submit button: disabled when form invalid OR status==='loading'
onSubmit calls store.submit(data) — store status prevents double-submit
Display inline field errors from react-hook-form
No hardcoded strings — all labels/errors via useTranslation()
Export from src/features/withdraw/components/index.ts

description: Build UI state components: LoadingState, ErrorState (with retry), SuccessState
steps: Create src/features/withdraw/ui/LoadingState.tsx — spinner + 'Processing...' i18n key
Create src/features/withdraw/ui/ErrorState.tsx — displays error.message, Retry button (calls store.retry() which reuses same idempotencyKey for network errors, resets key for others), Back button
Create src/features/withdraw/ui/SuccessState.tsx — shows withdrawal id, amount, destination, status from store.withdrawal
All strings via useTranslation()
Export from src/features/withdraw/ui/index.ts

description: Build WithdrawWidget — orchestrates form + state transitions
steps: Create src/features/withdraw/widgets/WithdrawWidget.tsx
Read status from useWithdrawStore()
Render: status==='idle'|'error' → <WithdrawForm/> + <ErrorState/> if error, status==='loading' → <LoadingState/>, status==='success' → <SuccessState/>
Use React.memo on sub-components to reduce re-renders
Export from src/features/withdraw/widgets/index.ts

description: Wire Withdraw page (app/withdraw/page.tsx) and apply design system styles
steps: Create app/withdraw/page.tsx — server component, renders <WithdrawWidget/> centered on page
Create src/features/withdraw/withdraw.module.scss — layout styles using CSS tokens from design system
Apply glassmorphism card style, dark background, premium typography per design-system output
Add metadata: title='Withdraw | USDT', description for SEO
Verify: npm run dev, page renders at /withdraw with correct layout

description: Polish UI — animations, hover states, responsive, accessibility
steps: Add CSS transitions on button hover/active states (150-300ms)
Add loading spinner animation via @keyframes in SCSS
Ensure form inputs have associated <label> elements with htmlFor
Add focus-visible styles for keyboard navigation
Test responsive at 375px, 768px, 1024px — no horizontal scroll
Check color contrast >= 4.5:1 for all text
Add @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) to disable animations
Verify: npm run build, no stylelint errors

description: Optional: persist last withdrawal to sessionStorage (5-min TTL) for reload recovery
steps: In useWithdrawStore, add zustand persist middleware with sessionStorage and a custom TTL check
On store hydration, if stored withdrawal is older than 5 min, clear it
Show restored withdrawal in SuccessState on reload
Verify: submit form, reload page, SuccessState still shows the withdrawal

description: Write unit tests — happy-path submit, API error, double-submit guard
steps: Install: npm install -D jest @testing-library/react @testing-library/jest-dom @testing-library/user-event jest-environment-jsdom ts-jest
Configure jest.config.ts with jsdom environment and moduleNameMapper for SCSS
Test 1 (happy-path): render WithdrawForm, fill valid amount+destination+confirm, submit, assert store.status becomes 'success' and SuccessState renders withdrawal id
Test 2 (API 409 error): mock createWithdrawal to throw ConflictError, submit, assert ErrorState renders 409 message
Test 3 (double-submit): submit once (mock slow API), attempt second submit while loading, assert createWithdrawal called exactly once
Run: npx jest --coverage, all 3 tests pass

description: Optional: E2E test with Playwright — full happy-path and 409 flow
steps: Install: npm install -D @playwright/test, run npx playwright install chromium
Create tests/e2e/withdraw.spec.ts
Test 1 (happy-path): navigate /withdraw, fill form, submit, assert success state shows withdrawal id
Test 2 (409): intercept POST route and force 409 response, submit, assert error message visible
Run: npx playwright test, all tests pass

description: Write README.md — setup, run, key decisions, prod auth approach
steps: Document: npm install, npm run dev, npm test, npx playwright test
Key decisions: idempotency_key strategy, retry vs reset logic, zustand over Context, sessionStorage TTL
Prod auth approach: access token in HttpOnly cookie set by server, never exposed to JS; use Next.js middleware for route protection
Security notes: no dangerouslySetInnerHTML, no localStorage tokens, zod schema validation on both client and API route
Verify: README.md renders correctly on GitHub

description: Final code review — run frontend-code-review validation scripts
steps: Run: npm run lint (ESLint) — 0 errors
Run: npx stylelint 'src/**/*.scss' — 0 errors
Run: npx tsc --noEmit — 0 errors
Run: npx jest — all tests pass
Check: no console.log, no 'any' type, no unused variables, no hardcoded user-visible strings
Check: every folder has index.ts re-exporting its public API
Verify: npm run build exits 0 with no warnings
